21.correct: The speed of sound in air at 0 degrees Celsius is about 331 m/s.

wrong.A: 344 m/s is the speed at about 20-22 C, not at 0 C.
wrong.C: 316 m/s is too low for 0 C.
wrong.D: 346 m/s corresponds to a warmer temperature.

21.fact: The speed of sound in air increases by about 0.6 m/s for each 1 C rise in temperature.

22.correct: The Ladakh plateau is called the 'Roof of the World' due to its very high elevation.

wrong.B: The Deccan Plateau is a lower peninsular plateau.
wrong.C: The Chotanagpur Plateau is in eastern India at modest elevation.
wrong.D: The Malwa Plateau is in central India.

22.fact: The Ladakh region is a cold desert lying between the Karakoram and the Himalayas.

23.correct: The 8th BRICS Summit (2016) was held in Goa, India.

wrong.D: Beijing did not host the 8th summit.

23.fact: At the Goa summit, BRICS leaders also met with BIMSTEC leaders.

24.correct: A positive effect of liberalization and globalization on Indian industry was access to advanced technology and entry into larger (global) markets.

wrong.A: Competition raised efficiency rather than reducing it.
wrong.C: It increased competition for Indian firms, not their monopoly.
wrong.D: It did not mean the state fully withdrew from infrastructure.

24.fact: Post-1991 liberalization integrated India more deeply with the world economy.

25.correct: Between 1950 and 1980, heavy industry, steel and power were considered the 'commanding heights' of the economy, reserved largely for the public sector.

wrong.B: Handicrafts were not the commanding heights.
wrong.C: Agriculture, though vital, was not termed the commanding heights.

25.fact: The 'commanding heights' idea came from the 1956 Industrial Policy Resolution.

26.correct: Technological progress that replaces workers with machines creates structural unemployment.

wrong.A: Seasonal unemployment is linked to seasons (e.g. farming), not technology.
wrong.B: Disguised unemployment is surplus labour with near-zero productivity (common in agriculture).
wrong.C: Frictional unemployment is the short-term gap while changing jobs.

26.fact: Structural unemployment arises from a mismatch between workers' skills and available jobs.

27.correct: As announced in the Union Budget 2025, the SWAMIH Fund-2 was allocated a total corpus of about Rs 15,000 crore.
27.fact: SWAMIH funds help complete stalled affordable and mid-income housing projects.

28.correct: On 9 October 2024 the Union Cabinet approved continuing the supply of fortified rice (under the food schemes) up to December 2028.

wrong.A: 15 August 2024 is not the approval date.
wrong.D: 1 January 2025 is not the approval date.

28.fact: Rice fortification adds iron, folic acid and vitamin B12 to combat anaemia and malnutrition.

29.correct: In Tamil Bhakti literature, the Vaishnava saints (devotees of Vishnu) were called the Alvars.

wrong.A: 'Yogi' is a general term, not the name of these saints.
wrong.C: The Nayanars were the Shaiva (Shiva-devotee) saints.
wrong.D: Tirthankaras are the spiritual teachers of Jainism.

29.fact: The Alvars and Nayanars led the Bhakti movement in South India.

30.correct: The 'Lepcha' (Lebना/folk) dance is associated with Sikkim, the home of the Lepcha community.

wrong.B: It is not from West Bengal.

30.fact: The Lepchas are among the indigenous inhabitants of Sikkim.

31.correct: Jammu & Kashmir began geo-tagging of Chinar trees as a step to preserve its natural heritage.

wrong.B: This option does not name the correct UT.
wrong.C: Puducherry did not start this Chinar initiative.
wrong.D: Andaman and Nicobar is not associated with Chinar trees.

31.fact: The Chinar is an iconic tree of Kashmir, famous for its red autumn foliage.

32.correct: In India, a 'main worker' is a person who works for at least 183 days (about six months) in a year.

wrong.B: 90 days is below the main-worker threshold.
wrong.C: 100 days corresponds to MGNREGA's guarantee, not the main-worker definition.

32.fact: Those who work for less than 183 days are classified as 'marginal workers' in the Census.

33.correct: The festival of Eid-ul-Fitr is celebrated to mark the end of the fasting month of Ramzan in Islam.

wrong.A: Bakrid (Eid-ul-Adha) is the festival of sacrifice, later in the year.
wrong.C: Muharram marks the Islamic new year and mourning.
wrong.D: Shab-e-Barat is a separate night of forgiveness.

33.fact: Eid-ul-Fitr begins with a special prayer and the giving of charity (Zakat al-Fitr).

34.correct: Birsa Munda was born into a Munda tribal family that lived in the Chotanagpur (plateau) region.

wrong.A: 'Dhar' is not the region associated with Birsa Munda.
wrong.B: Jhabua is in Madhya Pradesh, not Birsa's region.
wrong.C: This option does not name his region.

34.fact: Birsa Munda led the 'Ulgulan' (Great Tumult) tribal rebellion against the British; his birth anniversary is Janjatiya Gaurav Divas.

35.correct: Both statements are correct: synthetic fibre (I) is an example of a chemical-based industry, and salt, sulphur and potash industries (II) also use natural minerals - so 'Both I and II' is right.

wrong.A: 'Only I' omits the correct second statement.
wrong.B: 'Only II' omits the correct first statement.
wrong.D: 'Neither' is wrong since both statements are correct.

35.fact: Chemical industries are classified into organic and inorganic branches.

36.correct: Sachin Tendulkar scored his 100th international century in 2012 (against Bangladesh in the Asia Cup).

wrong.C: 2014 is after he retired from international cricket (2013).
wrong.D: 2011 is the year he reached 99 centuries, not 100.

36.fact: Sachin Tendulkar is the only cricketer to score 100 international centuries.

37.correct: Sushil Kumar won Olympic medals in both 2008 (bronze, Beijing) and 2012 (silver, London), so 'Both I and II' is correct.

wrong.D: 'Neither' is wrong since he won in both years.

37.fact: Sushil Kumar was the first Indian to win individual Olympic medals at two consecutive Games.

38.correct: 'Roman rings' and the 'vaulting table' are apparatus associated with gymnastics.
38.fact: The rings test upper-body strength and balance in men's artistic gymnastics.

39.correct: In the 2024 Global Hunger Index, India's score was 27.3, placing it in the 'serious' category.
39.fact: The Global Hunger Index measures hunger using undernourishment, child wasting, child stunting and child mortality.

40.correct: In the 2024 Environmental Performance Index, China was ranked around 156th.
40.fact: The Environmental Performance Index (EPI) is produced by Yale and Columbia universities.
